what_it_does: This file is a JSON registry that enumerates every kind of event a Claude Code session can emit and assigns each a render contract: where it is shown (placement), which UI component draws it, and which raw-event fields the UI needs (field_map). It also defines lookup rules, closed vocabularies, evidence tags, and fallback behavior for unlisted event types.
observations: A 'registry-is-truth' ledger with schema_ver 1 and a closed vocabulary for surfaces, placements, components, update_targets, stream_accumulators, and evidence.
Declarations are keyed by type/subtype, with family fallbacks using '*' (e.g. 'system/*', 'attachment/*').
Each declaration carries surface, placement, component, is_terminal, blocks_execution, evidence, field_map, and a plain-text summary.
Sub-dispatch rules are encoded in keys: 'assistant.content.<block.type>', 'user.content.<block.type>', 'stream_event/<inner>', and 'attachment/<type>'.
Some entries are marked as observed in real stream captures or transcript corpus, others as inferred or atlas-docs only.
The lookup law is explicit: exact match -> family fallback -> bare type -> UNDECLARED, with UNDECLARED events still rendered as UnknownEvent and recorded as a registry gap.
Terminal events (e.g. 'result/success') close the working state; some events (e.g. 'auth_status', 'assistant.content.tool_use.AskUserQuestion') set blocks_execution true.
Supervisor-native wire events ('init', 'text', ...) appear at the end with their own surface.
The file is truncated mid-declaration at the end (the 'text' supervisor event is incomplete).
standouts: The 'gap-pressure law': constrained operation is a sensor for its registry — family-fallback hits render but also record a registry-gap drop.
The lookup law with exact / family / bare / UNDECLARED cascade, guaranteeing no event is silently dropped.
Evidence tagging per declaration (observed-stream, observed-transcript, atlas-docs, inferred, family-fallback, supervisor-native).
The distinction between stream and transcript surfaces for the same logical concept (e.g. system hook events vs attachment/hook_* events).
The 'system/thinking_tokens' entry, noted as a real stream emit absent from docs.
conventions: Uses a flat JSON object keyed by declaration names, with a top-level 'vocab' enum section.
Field maps express UI output fields as dotted paths into the raw event.
Family fallbacks use a literal '*' suffix and are explicitly marked as family-fallback evidence.
Booleans is_terminal and blocks_execution are present on every declaration.
Evidence is a single string from a closed vocabulary rather than a list.
Comments and rationale are stored in 'summary' fields rather than JSON comments.
concerns: The file is truncated at the end, so the supervisor 'text' declaration and any following entries are incomplete.
Some entries are based on inference or atlas docs rather than observed data ('system/session_state_changed', 'assistant.content.redacted_thinking'), which could drift from actual wire format.
A few field_maps look thin for rich content types, e.g. 'user.content.image' only maps media_type and omits the actual source/path data.
There is potential overlap between stream system hook events and transcript attachment hook events that is not reconciled in this file.
notes: This is the central render-contract ledger: it tells the UI what to draw for every session event and makes missing declarations visible through gap-pressure rather than silent drops.
questions: What is the complete set of supervisor-native declarations after the truncated 'text' entry?
How does the runtime resolve field_map dotted paths when intermediate keys are missing or the shape differs?
Are stream and transcript surfaces mutually exclusive for the same logical event, or can they both fire for one occurrence?
What is the exact schema and behavior of 'system/session_state_changed' since it is marked inferred?
How are registry-gap drops surfaced to operators or registry maintainers?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a closed-vocabulary render-declaration registry with lookup law and gap-pressure behavior.
apparent_themes: Constrained operation as a sensor for registry gaps
Explicit render contracts separating data from presentation
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the UI's rendering behavior deterministic, versioned, and auditable by declaring every event type with its presentation contract, while using fallback rendering to keep the system resilient and to detect where the registry is incomplete.
novelty: The explicit 'gap-pressure' law and the requirement that family-fallback hits both render and record a registry-gap drop is unusual; most registries either silently fall back or fail closed.
contribution: A structured, evidence-tagged render-declaration schema plus a lookup law that ensures every session event is rendered and that missing declarations are surfaced as registry gaps rather than hidden.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON registry of Claude Code session event types with render contracts specifying placement, UI component, and field mappings, plus a lookup law that falls back through exact, family, and bare keys while recording registry gaps.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a closed-vocabulary, evidence-based source of truth for how every session event should be rendered and to make missing declarations visible rather than silently dropping events.
